svg.text: • Text editor "find": grep, ripgrep, vim use Boyer-Moore-style by default; KMP for short patterns.
• Network intrusion detection: Snort, Suricata signature matching (often Aho-Corasick).
• Bioinformatics: exact substring search in DNA / protein sequences.
• Spam filters / antivirus: match known signatures against streams.
• Plagiarism / duplicate detection: exact match between document fragments.
• Compiler lexers: keyword and operator matching in tokenizers.
• Streaming logs / observability: fixed-pattern alerting in low-memory environments.
When to pick KMP vs alternatives
• Worst-case linearity matters → KMP (Boyer-Moore can be O(n·m) on adversarial inputs).
• Single short pattern, ASCII text → Boyer-Moore or memmem (libc) usually faster in practice.
• Many patterns at once → Aho-Corasick (KMP's natural extension to a trie).
• Streaming, no random access → KMP (text pointer never moves backward).
• Period / borders / suffix structure of strings → KMP failure array is the data structure.
• Very small alphabet (binary) → KMP / Z dominate; Boyer-Moore's skip heuristic is weaker.
KMP is rarely the fastest, but it's the predictable, contention-free baseline.
